description: Calculate the dipping ranges (geographical visual ranges) for two Solent lighthouses and create a calibration scenario in Bridge Command at /opt/bridgecommand/Scenarios/Landfall_Calibration/. Use the formula Distance (nm) = 2.03 * (sqrt(H) + sqrt(h)), where H is object height (m) and h is observer eye height (m). For St. Catherine's Point Light (50° 34.55' N, 001° 17.60' W, H=41m), calculate the range for Own Ship (h=12m) and determine the position exactly 180° True (South) from the light at this distance. For The Needles Light (50° 39.73' N, 001° 35.50' W, H=24m), calculate the range for a Target Vessel (h=8m) and determine the position exactly 270° True (West) from the light at this distance. The scenario must include an environment.ini (Solent setting, 23:00, visibility >=25nm, calm weather), an ownship.ini (placed at the St. Catherine's calculated position, heading 000), and an othership.ini (one target vessel at the Needles calculated position, heading 090). Note that 1 nm equals 1 minute of Latitude, and for Longitude, use Departure = dLong * Cos(Lat) or estimate 1 minute Longitude as approximately 0.63 nm at this latitude.
